title: In-memory WorkflowState store: replace per-session + map Rdict in workflow.py
description: Replace the two speedict (Rdict) backends in fastworkflow/workflow.py with a process-global, thread-safe in-memory store (dict + RLock). title: Remove speedict/RocksDB from the Workflow hot path (Blueprint/Session-State split)
description: Workflow.create does ~6 RocksDB (speedict Rdict) open/close cycles per new session (per-session snapshot DB + global workflowid_2_sessiondata map, x2 for cme+app), dominating per-request latency while payload is trivial. Definitions are already cached per folderpath. Move session state to a process-global in-memory store, removing speedict from the hot path. Opt-in disk persistence dropped (CLI re-run context parity accepted as lost; run_fastapi_mcp owns durability via SessionStateStore+ConversationStore). speedict KEPT elsewhere (enablecache, ConversationStore, NLU clarification cache). Non-breaking for public Workflow API + consumers. Ship as v2.21.4.
close_reason: Closed

title: Remove speedict/RocksDB from the Workflow hot path (Blueprint/Session-State split)
description: Workflow.create currently does ~6 RocksDB (speedict Rdict) open/close cycles per new session (per-session snapshot DB + global workflowid_2_sessiondata map, x2 for cme+app workflow), dominating per-request latency (~90-270ms) while the payload is trivial (<0.1ms). The heavy definitions (RoutingRegistry, command_directory, context model) are already cached per folderpath, so Workflow.create's entire cost is RocksDB instance churn. This epic separates the immutable Workflow definition (cached blueprint) from mutable per-session state by moving session state to a process-global in-memory store, removing speedict from the hot path entirely. The opt-in disk persistence backend is intentionally dropped (CLI re-run context parity is accepted as lost; run_fastapi_mcp already owns durability via SessionStateStore + ConversationStore and overwrites workflow context on recreate). speedict is KEPT elsewhere (enablecache decorator, ConversationStore, NLU clarification cache). Must be non-breaking for the public Workflow API and all consumers. Ship as v2.21.4.

title: Endpoints: return TurnOutput from invoke_agent / invoke_assistant / perform_action

Update the non-streaming turn endpoints in fastworkflow/run_fastapi_mcp/__main__.py to serialize TurnOutput instead of CommandOutput.

CHANGES (per endpoint):
- invoke_agent() (line ~757): turn_output = await run_process_turn(...); response_data = turn_output.model_dump(mode='json'); attach traces (additive key) as today; return JSONResponse(response_data). Keep lock/409, 404, save_conversation_incremental, collect_trace_events.

title: WEC: add process_action_turn() returning TurnOutput for direct actions
description:
Direct actions (perform_action endpoint) and startup actions must become their own logical turn returning a TurnOutput, mirroring process_turn() for messages.

CONTEXT: fastworkflow/workflow_execution_context.py already has process_action(action)->CommandOutput (line 579) which calls _begin_turn() and _process_action(), so the turn accumulator (_turn_outputs) is populated. It just never builds/returns a TurnResult/TurnOutput.

CHANGES:
- Add WorkflowExecutionContext.process_action_turn(action) -> fastworkflow.TurnOutput (parallel to process_turn at line 485). It runs the same dispatch as process_action() and then returns self._build_turn_result(command_output).turn_output.
- Prefer refactoring process_action() internals into a shared _execute_action() (like _execute_message() at line 498) so both the legacy CommandOutput method and the new TurnOutput method share dispatch. Keep legacy process_action() returning CommandOutput for back-compat within this release window.
- Verify _build_turn_result() (line 525) handles the action path: a direct action is COMPLETED (never awaiting_user; _awaiting_user is False), answer = command_output.command_responses[0].response, success computed from command_outputs.

title: utils.py: route turn helpers through process_turn() and return TurnOutput
description:
Convert the executor helpers in fastworkflow/run_fastapi_mcp/utils.py from process_message()->CommandOutput to process_turn()->TurnOutput.

CHANGES:
- run_process_message(): call ctx.process_turn(message) instead of ctx.process_message(message); return fastworkflow.TurnOutput. Rename to run_process_turn (update call sites in __main__.py invoke_agent/invoke_assistant). Keep the asyncio.wait_for timeout + 504 behavior unchanged.
- run_process_message_with_trace_stream(): same swap; the executor future runs ctx.process_turn(message); final return value is a TurnOutput. Trace-queue draining via ctx.command_trace_queue is unchanged. Note the trace_queue-is-None fallback path also calls run_process_message -> keep consistent.
- persist_pending_after_turn(): accept the TurnOutput. Replace the _is_awaiting_user_output(CommandOutput) check with status-based detection: pending == ctx.awaiting_user or turn_output.status == TurnStatus.AWAITING_USER. Save/clear durable suspended state as before.

- Imports: add TurnOutput/TurnStatus from fastworkflow.

NOTE: process_turn() avoids the DeprecationWarning that process_message() emits. conversation_history is still populated by process_turn via _execute_message, so save_conversation_incremental continues to work unchanged.

DEPENDENCIES: none (foundational). Endpoint + streaming tasks depend on this.

title: Migrate run_fastapi_mcp transport edge to process_turn() / TurnOutput
description:
Migrate the run_fastapi_mcp transport edge from the deprecated WorkflowExecutionContext.process_message() (returns CommandOutput) to process_turn() (returns the public TurnOutput) so every turn endpoint returns a TurnOutput-based response.

CURRENT STATE (investigated 2026-06-15, verified against source):
- run_fastapi_mcp ALREADY uses WorkflowExecutionContext (WEC) per channel (Topology B). ChannelRuntime.execution_context is a WEC; sessions managed by ChannelSessionManager. Good baseline.
- BUT all turn paths still call the DEPRECATED ctx.process_message()/ctx.process_action() via utils helpers (run_process_message, run_process_action, run_process_message_with_trace_stream, _run_startup_sync). process_message() emits a DeprecationWarning (workflow_execution_context.py:477).
- Endpoints (invoke_agent, invoke_agent_stream, invoke_assistant, perform_action) serialize CommandOutput.model_dump(mode='json') and graft a 'traces' key. initialize() returns InitializeResponse.startup_output: Optional[CommandOutput].
- WEC.process_turn(message) already exists (workflow_execution_context.py:485) and returns the public TurnOutput. It calls _execute_message() then _build_turn_result().turn_output. _execute_message() still populates conversation_history, so conversation persistence (new_conversation/post_feedback/activate_conversation) keeps working.
- TurnOutput (fastworkflow/turn.py:168): turn_key, status: TurnStatus, failure_reason, answer: str, command_outputs: list[CommandOutput], plus computed success = all(command_outputs succeeded).
- process_action() (workflow_execution_context.py:579) returns CommandOutput and does NOT yet have a TurnOutput-returning sibling (no process_action_turn exists).
- Awaiting-user is detected today via _is_awaiting_user_output() reading command_responses[0].artifacts['awaiting_user'] OR ctx.awaiting_user (utils.py:354-373).

TARGET (per docs/turn_result_design_final.md sections 1a, 8, 14 — the v3.0 transport-edge cutover):
- Endpoints + SSE/NDJSON return TurnOutput.model_dump() (slim public projection, NOT internal TurnResult), with 'traces' still attached as an additive key.
- Awaiting-user detection switches to TurnOutput.status == TurnStatus.AWAITING_USER (plus ctx.awaiting_user) for durable pending persistence.
- MCP tool results map isError = not TurnOutput.success.
- Direct actions (perform_action) and startup become their own logical turn returning a TurnOutput projection.

THIS IS A BREAKING WIRE CHANGE: response shape goes from {command_responses, workflow_name, command_name, success, ...} to {turn_key, status, failure_reason, answer, command_outputs, success}. Clients and tests must be updated. Backward compat for these shapes is explicitly NOT required pre-v3.0 (spec section 1a).

KEY FILES: fastworkflow/run_fastapi_mcp/utils.py, fastworkflow/run_fastapi_mcp/__main__.py, fastworkflow/run_fastapi_mcp/mcp_specific.py, fastworkflow/workflow_execution_context.py, fastworkflow/turn.py, tests/test_fastapi_service.py, tests/test_fastapi_topology_b.py, docs/fastworkflow_fastapi_spec.md.

Tasks are file/concern-scoped. NO COMMITS until user approval.

title: R40: Define turn-level success semantics for TurnResult
description:
FINDING: TurnResult has neither a success field nor a derivation, while consumers need one (to_mcp_result sets isError from success; CLI and HTTP clients want a verdict). Agent mode makes it non-trivial: a good final answer can follow failed tool calls, so all(outputs.success) is wrong and answer.success is never set on the agent path. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) define success = did the turn produce a usable answer (agent: loop completed; deterministic: command success), distinct from per-execution success in the gallery - confirm; (2) how the replacement for to_mcp_result derives isError; (3) expose as computed_field on the wire (with R33). Depends on the R11 status enum. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R40.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar). (1) TurnResult.success = answer.success. Assistant mode: the executed command's own success flag (extraction errors and business-logic failures read False). Agent mode: True when the agent delivers an answer - crashes are already status=failed (A5). Recovery case (failed intermediate tool calls, correct final answer) reads True; per-execution success stays individually visible in the gallery. Documented explicitly: success != all commands succeeded (the old all() fold is NOT lifted to turn level). (2) ITERATION EXHAUSTION IS A QUALITY SIGNAL: when the ReAct loop ends by max_iters rather than finish (utils/react.py runs until finish, max_iters, or AskUserSuspend; the best-effort answer is today indistinguishable from a confident finish), the turn is stamped completed + success=False. Answer still enters records and the completed-only memory projection; the loop surfaces an exhausted flag to _finalize_agent_output. (3) WIRE EXPOSURE: success is serialized as a computed_field on TurnResult (HTTP/SSE visible for the first time; today's property never serializes); MCP isError = not success. Pre-decides the success half of R33; R33's remaining scope is the other predicates. Recorded in design doc Amendment A6 and review doc R40.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 two-stamps/recovery/polite-failure/giving-up): all three recommended options chosen. Cross-refs: A5 (failed turns), A4 (memory projection), R33 (partially pre-decided).
close_reason: Finding finalized: success=answer.success, max-iters exhaustion stamps success=False, success serialized with MCP isError=not success; recorded in bead, review doc R40, design doc Amendment A6

title: R8: Define TurnResult coverage for process_action and MCP paths
description:
FINDING: WEC.process_action exists (workflow_execution_context.py:377, _process_action at 664), is exposed via /perform_action, and appends to conversation_history like the other paths - but the design specifies the TurnResult contract only for process_message. Unrecorded action invocations would interleave invisible state mutations between recorded turns, corrupting the audit trail. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) does process_action return a TurnResult; (2) do action turns produce review records; (3) what is their user_message; (4) if excluded, state it explicitly in the design. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R8.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar) - FULL TURNS. process_action (the external /perform_action door; MCP rides the same endpoints) returns a TurnResult and writes a turn record like any other turn. SHAPE: command_outputs = [the one execution] with full provenance; answer = command_outputs[0].command_response (assistant-path aliasing, R10 copy-on-serialize applies); status per A3; success = the command's own success per A6; user_message = empty string (stated convention - no user words existed; the action is fully described by its command entry). RATIONALE: (a) closes the audit hole - the state-MUTATING path can never be the unrecorded one; (b) preserves today's agent-memory behavior under A1 rebuild-from-records (verified: _process_action appends to conversation_history today at workflow_execution_context.py:715 - without a record that note vanishes on restart); (c) one wire shape across all endpoints (feeds R3). Opt-out flag variant rejected to keep the audit guarantee unconditional. The INTERNAL CommandExecutor.perform_action helper (plumbing inside a turn) is unaffected. Recorded in design doc Amendment A11 and review doc R8.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 front-door/side-door): full turns, recommended option. Key new argument since the finding was written: A1's memory-rebuild-from-records makes recording side-door visits REQUIRED to avoid a silent memory regression.
close_reason: Finding finalized: process_action invocations are full turns with records, empty user_message; recorded in bead, review doc R8, design doc Amendment A11

title: R7: Decide the data-sensitivity posture of durable review persistence
description:
FINDING: the design flips payloads (access-review CSVs - identity/entitlement data) from ephemeral to durably-persisted-by-default with retention declared out of scope. For the xray domain this is a compliance posture change, not an infra detail. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) review persistence config flag and its default (on or off); (2) granularity - deployment-wide, per-workflow, or per-command payload opt-out (record persists, payload marked not-retained); (3) extend the 7.8 contract to name encryption-at-rest and access-control expectations for both stores. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R7.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar) - NO SWITCHES; RETENTION IS THE COMPLIANCE STORY. (1) Turn records AND payloads always persist; no opt-out configuration exists. Reviewer's opt-out recommendation rejected: post-A1 the records are load-bearing (conversation system of record + agent-memory source), and payload durability is the product. Consequence: the review-persistence-disabled fallback clauses in A4.2 and A5.4 are DEAD and superseded (struck through in the design doc) - cancel and abandon always record, payloads always transfer to their terminal record. (2) Compliance = age-based retention, INFRA-EXECUTED (decision 19 affirmed): deployments remove conversations older than their chosen age via conversation-prefix deletes; A8 makes this a single-operation co-GC (disk: directory-tree removal; redis: prefix SCAN+DEL). The framework ships no retention code or schedule; the documented contract is the deliverable - the review's contract-nobody-implements concern is accepted as a deployment responsibility with eyes open. (3) Security contract extends 7.8 (stated): both stores declared to hold PII/entitlement-grade data; deployment obligations = encryption at rest, TLS to redis, least-privilege service identity; framework obligations = record-mediated payload access only (A8), record contents never logged. Recorded in design doc Amendment A12 and review doc R7.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 + A1 reframing of records as load-bearing): Dhar rejected the switch premise entirely - everything persists, compliance via age-based retention. Retention owner = infra-delegated (decision 19 status quo), framework-owned lazy/sweeper options declined. A4.2/A5.4 fallback clauses reconciled.
close_reason: Finding finalized: no persistence switches, infra-executed age-based retention, security contract documented; recorded in bead, review doc R7, design doc Amendment A12

title: R5: Specify the artifact serialization and payload offload contract
description:
FINDING: section 8.3 is not implementable as written. Undefined: which artifact values get offloaded (keys? size threshold?); how a handle is distinguished from a legitimate string on read (an envelope like {__fw_payload_ref__, content_type, size} is required); policy for non-JSON-serializable artifact values (xray stashes actions objects; default=str mangles them); and command_parameters is declared str but carries a Pydantic model at runtime (workflow_agent.py:103-106 calls model_dump on it). OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) offload rule - recommend any str/bytes value above a configurable threshold (~4KB); (2) envelope format; (3) reject-loud vs stringify-with-marker for non-serializable values; (4) fix command_parameters declared type to str-or-dict. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R5.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar). (1) OFFLOAD RULE = SIZE THRESHOLD: any str/bytes artifact value larger than a configurable threshold (default ~4KB, env var) is offloaded to the turn-scoped PayloadStore (A8) and replaced in place by the envelope; smaller values stay inline. App-agnostic, no key-naming convention. (2) ENVELOPE CONVENTION: {"__fw_payload_ref__": <scoped handle>, "size": <bytes>, "content_type": <best-effort>}; marker key reserved; readers detect it to distinguish references from literals. (3) NON-SERIALIZABLE VALUES = STRICT REJECTION: record serialization raises with a clear error naming the offending artifact key and command; replaces today's silent json.dump(default=str) mangling (session_state_store.py:64). Verified safe during review: Action/Recommendation are typed fields distinct from artifacts and unused (R42/A9); xray's mapping packs ResponseTuple.actions into artifacts but that value is None in the verified flow (design doc 0.2/0.3); the framework's own cmd_parameters object in the NLU handshake never reaches serialization (consumed in-memory inside invoke_command). Contract: apps packing objects into artifacts must serialize them first in their own mapping code. (4) command_parameters HONESTY (stated convention): in-memory keeps the typed Pydantic instance; declared type corrected from the str lie; record serialization emits model_dump() dict. Store-level details (hash algo, compression, atomic writes) remain with R21. Recorded in design doc Amendment A10 and review doc R5.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 warehouse fine-print): size threshold + strict rejection. Dhar's clarification that Action/Recommendation are distinct from artifacts corrected the reviewer's overcautious breaks-xray warning - verified actions=None in the known flow, making strict rejection safe.
close_reason: Finding finalized: size-threshold offload, reserved-key envelope, strict rejection, honest command_parameters; recorded in bead, review doc R5, design doc Amendment A10

title: R6: Capture failed executions, failed turns, and abandoned turns
description:
FINDING: three observability holes. (1) _execute_workflow_query has no try/except around invoke_command (workflow_agent.py:76-98) so failed tool calls never enter command_outputs; (2) a turn that errors mid-agent-loop produces no review record at all; (3) abandoned suspended turns sit in the pending store forever and never reach review. The turns developers most need to inspect are exactly these. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) capture failed executions as CommandOutput(success=False) with exception detail in artifacts - confirm shape; (2) failure-path partial TurnResult write with status=failed - confirm; (3) abandoned turns: build a stale-pending sweep producing status=abandoned records, or document the gap deliberately. Depends on the R11 status enum. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R6.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-10 (with Dhar). (1) FAILED TOOL CALLS - capture with detail: _execute_workflow_query wraps invoke_command in try/except; on failure appends CommandOutput(success=False) with error type, message, truncated traceback in artifacts (traceback only in developer projection per R30), then re-raises so the ReAct loop formats the observation as today (utils/react.py:252). Suspension signals pass through: AskUserSuspend subclasses BaseException (immune); CommandCancelledError explicitly re-raised without capture. (2) FAILED TURNS - record + re-raise: process_message wraps the agent loop; fatal errors write a partial record (status=failed, executions so far, exception summary) then re-raise. Caller error behavior unchanged (HTTP 500s, transient-error retry semantics). Never-raise alternative rejected. (3) ABANDONED - TTL + lazy filing: a pending turn older than a configurable TTL is not resumed on next touch; it is filed as status=abandoned (payload handles transfer per A4) and the message starts a fresh turn. No background reaper needed for active channels; fix-6b4 covers never-touched channels as optional future work. Documented UX change: post-TTL answers start fresh. BONUS: closes R24's remaining path - every suspend-offloaded payload now ends up owned by a terminal record. Recorded in design doc Amendment A5 and review doc R6.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 waiter/logbook): all three recommended options chosen. Cross-refs: R30 (traceback in dev projection), A4 (payload transfer pattern), fix-6b4 (reaper rescoped to never-touched residue), R24 (now fully resolved across cancel+abandon paths).
close_reason: Finding finalized: capture-with-detail, record+re-raise, TTL+lazy abandon; recorded in bead, review doc R6, design doc Amendment A5

title: R3: Document and mitigate the wire-contract break (HTTP, SSE, MCP, external authors)
description:
FINDING: the bundled FastAPI server returns command_output.model_dump() directly in four endpoints (run_fastapi_mcp/__main__.py:798, 904, 1041, 1115), so command_responses is the JSON wire schema for /invoke_agent, the SSE final event, /invoke_assistant and /perform_action. The collapse plus TurnResult return breaks every HTTP/MCP client and every external workflow author, none of which section 11 mentions. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) one-release compat shim (model_validator accepting command_responses=[x] with DeprecationWarning) vs hard break; (2) API versioning approach (v2 endpoints, version field, or documented break); (3) deprecation window length. RECOMMENDATION on file: shim plus explicit versioned schema statement; ties into the R46 release train. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R3.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar). (1) AUTHOR SHIM WITH DEPRECATION WINDOW: CommandOutput gains a model_validator(mode=before) accepting the legacy command_responses=[x] keyword, mapping it to command_response, emitting DeprecationWarning. Hand-written workflows (xray's, external PyPI users') keep running across the upgrade; the legacy keyword is removed at the major release FOLLOWING its introduction (exact train per R46). (2) HTTP/SSE/MCP HARD-BREAK AT THE MAJOR: endpoints return the TurnResult shape; major version bump signals it. No /v2 endpoints, no dual-shape responses, no reverse-mapping to maintain. Consistent with R11/A3's no-dual-publish stance; appropriate because the bundled server's clients are largely org-controlled deployments and xray (which embeds the framework behind its own API) is unaffected on this surface. (3) STATED DELIVERABLE: a written schema-migration guide - old shape to new shape, field by field, covering HTTP response bodies, the SSE final event, the MCP result (isError = not success per A6), and the author-side constructor migration. Recorded in design doc Amendment A13 and review doc R3. Release sequencing finalized in R46.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 two-customer-groups: keyholders and label-readers): shim with window + wire hard break, both recommended options. Key context: xray is NOT an HTTP client of the bundled server (runs its own FastAPI, embeds WEC directly).
close_reason: Finding finalized: author shim with deprecation window, wire hard-break at the major, schema-migration guide deliverable; recorded in bead, review doc R3, design doc Amendment A13

title: R37: Reconcile with existing ConversationStore turn persistence (consolidate vs coexist)
description:
FINDING (design contradiction, largest): the framework ALREADY persists per-turn records durably. WEC maintains conversation_history (dspy.History) appended on agent, assistant and action paths with {conversation summary, conversation_traces, feedback}, and the bundled FastAPI server persists it after every turn via save_conversation_incremental into Rdict-backed ConversationStore. This falsifies design sections 7.1/7.2 (no per-turn durable write on happy path) and 9.1 (action_log has a sole consumer): the chain is action_log -> traces -> conversation_history -> agent cross-turn memory, durable store, and /post_feedback. As designed the framework would run three overlapping turn-persistence subsystems on three backend technologies. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) consolidate (TurnReviewStore becomes the system of record for turns; ConversationStore keeps only conversation-level metadata and turn-key lists; conversation_history rebuilt from review records) vs coexist with documented boundaries; (2) backend unification timing (Rdict vs disk/redis); (3) where conversation ids and feedback live (gates R9, R38, R47). RECOMMENDATION on file: consolidate. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R37.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-10 (with Dhar) - FULL ABSORPTION. (1) ConversationStore is eliminated as a subsystem; TurnReviewStore is not built under that name. A single unified ConversationTurnStore owns two record types: conversation metadata (key fw:conv:{channel}:{conv_id}; value topic/summary/status/timestamps/schema_version/metadata) and turn records (key fw:turn:{channel}:{conv_id}:{sortable-ts}-{uuid}; value = light TurnResult per R5/R25). (2) save_conversation_incremental is deleted - the once-per-logical-turn record write is the only durable turn write. (3) All eight consumers re-point: session restore and /activate_conversation rebuild dspy.History by projection from turn records (turn records are the source of truth for agent cross-turn memory; in-memory History is a per-session cache; turn records must always carry the projection fields); /list_conversations reads metadata records; /new_conversation and the shutdown hook finalize metadata and open the next; /dump_conversations iterates the keyspace; /post_feedback per R38. (4) Conversation id becomes a structural component of every turn key (resolves the R9 namespace question); with R2 turn-scoped payload keys, deleting a conversation prefix co-GCs metadata, turns and payloads under one lifecycle. (5) Backend: disk/redis split, factory mirroring get_session_state_store; Rdict exits the conversation subsystem. (6) Migration: accept loss - existing Rdict conversation data is neither read nor migrated; no legacy read path. Recorded in docs/turn_result_design.md Amendments A1 and docs/turn_result_design_review.md R37.
notes: Q and A: architecture = full absorption (after elaboration of the 8 ConversationStore consumers and why elimination-by-absorption is feasible); memory rebuild source = turn records; backend = disk/redis pair; migration = accept loss. Unblocks R9 (fix-vof.12), R38 (fix-vof.15), R47 (fix-vof.47).
close_reason: Finding finalized: full-absorption decision recorded in bead design field, review doc R37, and design doc Amendment A1

title: R42: Define policy for next_actions and recommendations through the agent path
description:
FINDING (design contradiction): the original bug class survives for two of three structured channels. Nothing populates next_actions or recommendations on the synthesized answer (verified: _finalize_agent_output builds a bare CommandResponse), yet design section 5.4 motivates answer-as-CommandResponse by these fields. The gallery (10.3) surfaces only payload-bearing outputs, so commands returning actions but no payload are dropped exactly like payloads are today. OPEN QUESTIONS: choose (a) gallery includes outputs bearing ANY structured content; (b) answer aggregates next_actions/recommendations from the final command (documented last-command-wins); (c) declare them dead on the agent path and remove the 5.4 motivation. RECOMMENDATION on file: decide explicitly - silence is the only wrong option. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R42.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar). EMPIRICAL BASIS established first: next_actions and recommendations have ZERO producers and ZERO consumers in the framework, examples, and tests (grep-verified; only field declarations exist). xray's action buttons travel via artifacts (which the design preserves), and post-R1/A7 the durable record keeps complete CommandResponses so these fields are auto-preserved if they ever gain producers. DECISIONS: (1) Gallery rule = PAYLOAD-BEARING ONLY (user decision, conservative; against reviewer recommendation). Accepted + documented consequence: an output bearing only buttons/recommendations appears in the durable record but not the live gallery - currently unreachable (zero producers), recoverable from the record. (2) Agent answer's next_actions/recommendations STAY EMPTY; section 5.4's circular motivation corrected in place (answer is CommandResponse for its text+artifacts shape; buttons belong on gallery entries where provenance is clear). (3) GALLERY PROVENANCE (new sub-finding surfaced by Dhar during review): TurnResult preserves full per-entry provenance (command_name, command_parameters, workflow_name, context) but xray's ResponseTuple has no command-identity slot - provenance dies at that wire format. xray will initially embed provenance in response text; extending ResponseTuple is XRAY-REPO SCOPE, OUT OF SCOPE for fastWorkflow - documented as a note on the 10.3 mapping. Recorded in design doc Amendment A9 and review doc R42.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 attachments/buttons/suggestion-cards + gallery structure clarification): Dhar's clarifying question (is the gallery one lumped list?) surfaced the ResponseTuple provenance gap, now documented. Gallery rule = payload-only; answer buttons = empty + 5.4 fix; provenance = embed-in-text initially, xray scope.
close_reason: Finding finalized: fields verified unused, payload-only gallery kept, answer stays bare, provenance documented as xray scope; recorded in bead, review doc R42, design doc Amendment A9

title: R1: Capture ask_user exchanges and event interleaving in TurnResult
description:
FINDING (design contradiction): retiring action_log drops the ask_user Q&A records appended today in _post_ask_user_response (workflow_agent.py:232), and TurnResult cannot represent ordering between command executions and user exchanges. user_message (singular) is wrong for multi-exchange logical turns in both topologies. Contradicts decision 18 (zero regression) and degrades the LLM conversation summary. Topology A also enqueues clarification CommandOutputs directly, bypassing invoke_command. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) ordered event sequence (discriminated union CommandExecutionEvent / UserExchangeEvent) vs parallel lists with sequence indices; (2) user_message becomes user_messages, or first-message plus exchanges; (3) confirm _post_ask_user_response as the second capture choke point. RECOMMENDATION on file: event sequence with command_outputs kept as a derived view. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R1.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar - Dhar's simplification superseded the reviewer recommendation). (1) NO UserExchangeEvent, no discriminated union: ask_user is modeled as a command execution. Each exchange = CommandOutput(command_name=ask_user, command_parameters=<clarification question>, command_response.response=<user answer>) appended IN ORDER into the existing command_outputs list. TurnResult keeps its original shape (answer + command_outputs); interleaving solved with zero new types. Precedent: _ask_user_tool (workflow_agent.py:276) already constructs such a CommandOutput for the output queue. (2) UNANSWERED-QUESTION CONVENTION (satisfies A4/A5): entry appended at ask/suspend time with response empty and success=False; filled and success=True on answer via _post_ask_user_response (choke point in both topologies). Cancelled/abandoned records end with the unanswered question; suspended partials show whats pending. (3) user_message holds the ORIGINAL REQUEST ONLY; clarification replies live solely in their ask_user entries (R44 decides raw-vs-refined separately). (4) ROLE INVERSION DOCUMENTED: in ask_user entries, command_parameters is the agents utterance, response is the users; summary projection maps {agent_query,user_response} to parameters/response directly - decision 18 zero-regression claim restored. (5) Knock-ons: R29 timestamps uniform per CommandOutput; R28 trajectory stays an offloaded blob; R30 user projection includes ask_user via command_name filter. Recorded in design doc Amendment A7 and review doc R1.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 flight-logbook): Dhar rejected both offered shapes and proposed ask_user-as-CommandOutput, which is simpler and verified sound against all accumulated requirements (A4 unanswered, A5 partials, projections, precedent in _ask_user_tool). user_message = original only.
close_reason: Finding finalized: ask_user modeled as CommandOutput in the ordered command_outputs list (Dhar's design); recorded in bead, review doc R1, design doc Amendment A7

title: R2: Resolve content-addressing vs co-GC contradiction in PayloadStore
description:
FINDING (design contradiction): content-addressed handles are shared across turns and channels, so the decision-19 contract (co-GC record plus payloads under one lifecycle key) is unimplementable as stated. Also a tenancy risk: global handles enable a cross-tenant hash oracle, and bare-handle fetch could leak data between channels. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) choose turn-scoped payload keys (channel/turn_key/hash - recommended) vs TTL-refresh-on-put vs refcounting vs mark-and-sweep; (2) is cross-turn dedup worth preserving at all; (3) adopt the rule that payload access is always mediated through the referencing turn record. RECOMMENDATION on file: turn-scoped keys; rewrite decisions 13 and 19. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R2.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar). (1) TURN-SCOPED PAYLOAD KEYS: fw:payload:{channel}:{conv_id}:{turn_key}:{content_hash}. Every payload owned by exactly one turn - sharing impossible by construction, co-GC contradiction dissolves. PayloadStore.put gains scope params (channel, conversation, turn key); handle = the full scoped key, opaque to callers. Content-addressing survives only as the leaf segment for WITHIN-TURN retry idempotency. Cross-turn dedup deliberately abandoned (storage cheap, benefit speculative, tenant isolation becomes structural). (2) CO-GC IS LITERAL: conversation-prefix delete removes conv metadata (A1) + turn records + payloads in one stroke (disk: directory tree; redis: prefix SCAN+DEL). Decision 19's one-lifecycle-key = the conversation prefix - implementable as delegated. Reader still tolerates missing payloads. R21's delete-surface concern largely dissolves (prefix delete natural). (3) RECORD-MEDIATED ACCESS CONTRACT: payload reads always authorize the referencing turn record first (JWT channel binding; admin path per R32), then resolve handles found inside it; bare-handle fetch endpoints forbidden. Closes the hash-oracle / cross-tenant-probe class. Supersedes design decisions 13 and 19. Recorded in design doc Amendment A8 and review doc R2.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 warehouse/claim-tickets/fingerprints): turn-scoped keys + record-mediated access, both recommended options. Cross-refs: A1 keyspace synergy, R21 (delete surface mostly resolved), R32 (admin path), R24 (already closed).
close_reason: Finding finalized: turn-scoped payload keys, literal conversation-prefix co-GC, record-mediated access; recorded in bead, review doc R2, design doc Amendment A8

title: Enforce sticky routing / single-owner per channel for Topology B
description: Topology B serialization (run_fastapi_mcp) makes a channel's suspended session relocatable across pods, but nothing in-code enforces that only one pod owns a channel at a time. The app workflow domain context persists in embedded RocksDB (speedict), which is single-writer-per-directory: two pods serving the same channel concurrently will lock-conflict/corrupt. Today the code ASSUMES infra (load balancer/router) guarantees one-pod-per-channel. Add an ownership lease/lock (e.g. Redis-based lease keyed by channel_id) so a pod must acquire ownership before serving a channel, with safe handoff on failover/rescale. Note: even if domain state were moved off RocksDB, single-owner is still desirable due to per-process live command-context objects and concurrency control.
acceptance_criteria: A channel cannot be actively served by two pods simultaneously; ownership is acquired/released with safe failover handoff; documented routing contract; integration test simulating contended ownership.
notes: Surfaced during trajectory-serialization review. Related: docs/workflow_execution_context_migration.md (sticky routing section), fastworkflow/workflow.py RocksDB single-writer, fastworkflow/session_state_store.py.

title: Lifecycle pruning for abandoned in-memory app-workflow entries (leak)
description: In-memory session-state entries for app workflows accumulate until close() (mirrors today's unbounded on-disk leak for never-closed app workflows; e.g. unique channel_id per request). Add lifecycle pruning so the in-memory store is pruned when a live session is evicted/GC'd (e.g. WeakValueDictionary of live objects, or explicit removal on ChannelSessionManager eviction). Not required for v2.21.4 (no worse than current disk behavior).

description:
Confirm the conversation/feedback endpoints still work once turns flow through process_turn().

ENDPOINTS TO VERIFY (no logic change expected, but confirm + add coverage):

title: MCP: map isError = not TurnOutput.success for MCP tool results

Per docs/turn_result_design_final.md section 14 (v3.0): MCP tool results should set isError = not success.

title: CLI appears to hang on agent-mode ask_user (clarification enqueued without trace sentinel)
description: Code-reading evidence (found during turn-result review R43, 2026-06-11): Topology A blocking ask_user (workflow_agent.py:279) puts the clarification CommandOutput on command_output_queue but emits NO trace sentinel - the only sentinel emitter is _maybe_enqueue_trace_sentinel (workflow_execution_context.py:436), called only at the four turn-END sites. The CLI (run/__main__.py:236-262) drains the trace queue until a None sentinel BEFORE reading the output queue. Consequence: on agent-mode ask_user, the spinner waits for a sentinel that never comes while the worker thread blocks on user_message_queue - apparent deadlock; the question never prints. NEEDS RUNTIME VERIFICATION (run an agent workflow in the CLI and trigger ask_user). Fix: pair every mid-turn clarification enqueue with a trace sentinel so the CLI wakes, prints the question, and returns to the prompt. Under the v3.0 R43/A19 queue contract the same pairing rule applies to the awaiting_user partial TurnResult.

title: R46: Decide the release train (staged vs big-bang) and upgrade-time behavior
description:
FINDING: the design couples three independently-shippable changes - the capture/TurnResult bug fix, the durable review feature, and the command_responses collapse - into one breaking release; only the collapse is intrinsically breaking. Decision 22 also leaves runtime behavior on old pending blobs unspecified (in-flight suspended sessions exist at every deploy). OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) adopt the staged train (stage 1 minor: TurnResult + shim, fixes the xray bug; stage 2 minor: stores behind config + R37 consolidation; stage 3 major: remove command_responses and action_log) vs single major release; (2) confirm graceful degradation on SCHEMA_VERSION mismatch (treat pending as expired, clear, notify user) instead of an exception. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R46.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar). (1) TRAIN = QUICK-FIX MINOR + ONE MAJOR. The originally sketched three-stage train was retired during review: A1's accept-loss consolidation means the store work deletes user-visible conversation history, which must not hide inside a minor. ~v2.21 (minor, non-breaking): TurnResult type, capture machinery (ask_user per A7, failure capture per A5), NEW process_turn() method returning TurnResult, A13 constructor shim introduced with DeprecationWarnings (clock starts early); process_message unchanged; xray switches its runner to process_turn() - THE ORIGINAL PAYLOAD BUG IS FIXED HERE. v3.0 (major): process_message returns TurnResult (A3 awaiting_user-artifact removal lands with it), wire hard-break (A13), ConversationTurnStore consolidation with announced data loss (A1), action_log retired, command_responses removed from the model surface while the shim still accepts the legacy keyword. v4.0: shim removed (A13 window pinned: introduced 2.21, removed 4.0). (2) UPGRADE DAY = GRACEFUL EXPIRY: pending blob with old SCHEMA_VERSION is cleared on first touch; user told 'your previous question expired, please re-ask'; no record synthesized (old blob unreadable by definition). Migration guide documents this + optional pre-upgrade drain recommendation. Recorded in design doc Amendment A14 and review doc R46.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 renovation/move-out-weekend with updated map): quick-fix-minor+major and graceful expiry, both recommended options. Key reframing: three-stage retired because A1 data loss belongs in a major. R3's deprecation window now concretely pinned.
close_reason: Finding finalized: quick-fix minor (process_turn + shim) then one major, shim removed at v4.0; graceful expiry for old pending blobs; recorded in bead, review doc R46, design doc Amendment A14

title: R41: Disambiguate the live response path (in-memory payloads vs store handles)
description:
FINDING: section 10.1 offloads payloads at persistence; section 10.3 says the live xray mapping fetches payloads lazily from the PayloadStore by handle. One reading pays put-then-get per turn for bytes already in memory; the other (inline from memory) makes the lazily-by-handle language wrong and balloons bundled-server model_dump responses with all gallery payloads. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) live path maps from the in-memory TurnResult with handles reserved for the review reader (recommended), or serve live from handles for response-size control; (2) per-response payload size cap for the bundled server JSON/SSE bodies. Interacts with R45 eager offload. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R41.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar). (1) LIVE RESPONSE SERVED FROM RAM: the runner/mapping reads payloads from the in-memory TurnResult; offload happens on a serialized COPY at the persistence boundary (R10 copy-on-serialize), purely for review. Zero store reads on the hot path. Section 10.3's fetching-lazily-by-handle language corrected to apply ONLY to the review reader, never the live path. (2) BUNDLED-SERVER RESPONSES: payloads inline up to a configurable cap (MAX_INLINE_PAYLOAD, generous default ~10MB); above the cap the response carries the A10 envelope marked not-inlined-available-via-review-record (path depends on the future R32 read API, documented as such). (3) R45 CONSEQUENCE (noted here, finalized there): with the live path pinned to RAM, payloads must stay in memory until the response is built - eager offload buys no memory relief, so R45 collapses to boundary-offload with a documented memory profile. Recorded in design doc Amendment A16 and review doc R41.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 kitchen/warehouse with explicit R45 coupling): serve-from-RAM + configurable cap, both recommended options. R45 (fix-vof.43) is now mostly determined.
close_reason: Finding finalized: live path serves from RAM, configurable inline cap, handles are review-only; recorded in bead, review doc R41, design doc Amendment A16

title: R43: Define the command_output_queue / CLI contract after the redesign
description:
FINDING: the queue transport has its own contract the design never updates: _finalize_agent_output, _process_message and _process_action all enqueue the final CommandOutput; Topology A enqueues mid-turn clarifications directly; the CLI (run/__main__.py) consumes the queue and iterates command_responses. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) queue carries per-event CommandOutputs plus a terminal TurnResult (recommended - mirrors live-then-final), or TurnResult only; (2) enumerate ChatSession.keep_alive and CLI renderer changes in section 11. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R43.

title: R38: Give /post_feedback a home compatible with write-once review records
description:
FINDING: /post_feedback (run_fastapi_mcp/__main__.py:1276-1316) mutates the last in-memory conversation turn AFTER completion - after the write-once review record is sealed. Feedback is first-order observability data and the design never mentions it. OPEN QUESTIONS: choose (a) mutable feedback field on review records (violates write-once); (b) separate feedback keyspace keyed by turn_key (recommended); (c) feedback stays in ConversationStore with a turn_key link (fits R37 consolidation). See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R38.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar) - SEPARATE FEEDBACK RECORDS. Feedback is its own small record type in the unified keyspace: fw:feedback:{channel}:{conv_id}:{turn_key} holding score, comment, timestamp. (1) Turn records stay STRICTLY write-once - R16 enforcement and audit immutability hold with no carve-outs. (2) Same prefix scans serve everything: review reader and A1 memory rebuild fetch turns + feedback cards in one conversation-prefix scan; A8/A12 retention deletes cards with their envelopes automatically. (3) Conventions matching today: re-posting overwrites (last-write-wins on the single feedback key); /post_feedback targets the latest completed turn of the active conversation (arbitrary-turn feedback = future R32 read-API territory); feedback enters the agent-memory projection for completed turns exactly as the dspy.History feedback slot does today (A1 projection constraint satisfied via the read-time join). Recorded in design doc Amendment A18 and review doc R38.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 sealed-envelopes/sticky-notes): separate feedback records, recommended option. Original option (c) (ConversationStore home) was dead post-A1; its spirit maps to this resolution within the unified store.
close_reason: Finding finalized: separate feedback records in the unified keyspace, turn records stay write-once; recorded in bead, review doc R38, design doc Amendment A18

title: R39: Model cancelled turns (/cancel_pending)
description:
FINDING: /cancel_pending (run_fastapi_mcp/__main__.py:1133-1160) abandons a suspended ask_user turn and clears pending state - a fourth terminal state the design does not model. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) do cancelled turns write a review record with status=cancelled carrying the partial event sequence (recommended - it is real history); (2) who cleans up payloads offloaded at the suspend boundary, now referenced by nothing (ties to R24). Depends on the R11 status enum. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R39.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-10 (with Dhar). (1) RECORD, NOT SHRED: /cancel_pending and the A2 auto-cancel-on-switch paths write a turn record with status=cancelled under the turn's ORIGINAL conversation, carrying the partial event sequence (commands executed so far + the unanswered clarification question; event shape per R1) and the payload handles already offloaded at suspend. Payload ownership transfers from the pending blob to the cancelled record - no cleanup step needed; R24's orphan concern dissolves for the cancel path (abandoned path remains with R6.3). Sequence under per-session lock: serialize partial TurnResult (status=cancelled) -> write record -> clear pending blob. Today's cancel_pending() (workflow_execution_context.py:289) only resets in-memory state; it gains the record write. (2) R7 interplay: if review persistence is disabled by config, cancel falls back to shredding, and only that mode performs an explicit suspend-payload delete. (3) AGENT-MEMORY PROJECTION RULE (clarifies A1.2): rebuilt dspy.History projects from status=completed records ONLY; cancelled/failed/abandoned records are review-only and never enter agent working memory - matches today's behavior. Recorded in design doc Amendment A4 and review doc R39.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5): record vs shred = record (with R7 config fallback); memory projection = completed only. Cross-refs: R1 (unanswered-question event shape), R24 (cancel-path orphans dissolved), R6.3 (abandoned path still open), R7 (config switch).
close_reason: Finding finalized: cancelled turns recorded under original conversation; completed-only memory projection; recorded in bead, review doc R39, design doc Amendment A4

title: R10: Answer aliasing - copy-on-serialize rule and headline/gallery dedup
description:
FINDING: in the deterministic path answer IS command_outputs[-1].command_response (same object). In-place offload would corrupt the live TurnResult through both aliases; and the xray mapping would render the same payload twice (headline source and gallery entry). Blind deepcopy is also wrong - artifacts can hold live app objects and command_parameters holds a Pydantic model. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) confirm selective-copy serializer rule (no in-place mutation, no blind deepcopy); (2) confirm mapping dedup rule (skip gallery entries whose command_response is the answer object). See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R10.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar). (1) SELECTIVE COPY-ON-SERIALIZE (stated; implied by A10/A16): the serializer never mutates live TurnResult/CommandOutput objects and never blind-deep-copies them (artifacts can hold live application objects; command_parameters holds a typed model). It builds a new structure - small fields copied, values converted per A10's contract (threshold offload to envelope, model_dump for parameters, strict rejection otherwise) - originals untouched. A16's pristine-in-RAM live path depends on this. (2) HEADLINE NEVER CARRIES PAYLOADS (supersedes the reviewer's earlier identity-skip suggestion): the headline ResponseTuple is always narrative text + metadata; the gallery always contains ALL payload-bearing outputs in turn order. One uniform invariant across agent/assistant/action turns; the faithful reading of decision 21 (the UI, not the framework, picks the featured payload); deterministic-turn duplication eliminated by construction. Recorded in design doc Amendment A20 and review doc R10.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 one-object-two-names): headline-never-carries-payloads chosen - the reviewer's own recommendation updated mid-pass from identity-skip after testing it against decision 21's UI-decides intent.
close_reason: Finding finalized: selective copy-on-serialize, headline never carries payloads; recorded in bead, review doc R10, design doc Amendment A20

title: R11: Introduce first-class TurnResult.status (replace awaiting_user artifact sniffing)
description:
FINDING: suspension is signaled today by artifacts[awaiting_user]=True set in _awaiting_user_output (workflow_execution_context.py:518-520) and sniffed by the runner and utils.py:355-357 - a stringly-typed protocol. The redesign is the moment to replace it. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) confirm TurnStatus enum: completed, awaiting_user, failed, cancelled, abandoned; (2) confirm it replaces _output_is_awaiting_user and the runner branch condition; (3) scope - does the artifacts protocol get removed in the same release. Gates R6, R39, R40. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R11.

design: RESOLVED 2026-06-10 (with Dhar). (1) TurnResult.status: TurnStatus enum with ALL FIVE values: completed, awaiting_user, failed, cancelled, abandoned. cancelled is required by R9 auto-cancel-on-switch; abandoned is reserved now (written only if the R6.3 stale-pending sweep is built) so readers never need a schema bump. (2) The artifacts[awaiting_user] protocol is REMOVED IMMEDIATELY in the release that introduces TurnResult - no dual-publish window. _awaiting_user_output stops stamping the artifact; _output_is_awaiting_user sniffing (utils.py:354-357, xray runner) is deleted; the branch becomes turn.status == AWAITING_USER. Contained because every consumer of the old signal is in-repo or user-owned and is being rewritten for the TurnResult return type in the same release anyway; does not by itself force a big-bang release in R46. (3) NO FURTHER CLEANUP (user decision): all four CommandOutput predicates and the NLU-internal artifact handshake (command_handled / command_name / cmd_parameters between wildcard.py and CommandExecutor) carry into the redesigned model unchanged per design section 5.2. For the record: command_aborted and not_what_i_meant were verified to have zero consumers in framework+tests (definitions only, __init__.py:78,86); retained deliberately as public API. Formalizing the handshake is possible future work, out of scope. Recorded in design doc Amendment A3 and review doc R11.
notes: Q and A: enum = all five; artifact removal = immediate (user chose against dual-publish recommendation); cleanup scope = none (user chose conservative option after ELI5 of the sticky-note protocol and the dead-predicate evidence). Unblocks R6 (fix-vof.8), R39 (fix-vof.16), R40 (fix-vof.11).

title: R9: Add conversation scoping to the review namespace
description:
FINDING: one channel spans multiple conversations (/new_conversation, ConversationStore conversation ids), so TurnReviewStore.list(channel_id) interleaves turns across conversations with no way to scope a review. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) conversation id in the turn key vs required record metadata with filtered listing; (2) source of the id (ConversationStore owns the counter - interacts with R37); (3) define behavior when the conversation switches while a turn is suspended. See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R9.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-10 (with Dhar). (1) Namespace resolved structurally by R37/A1: conversation id is a key component of every turn record (fw:turn:{channel}:{conv_id}:{sortable-ts}-{uuid}); per-conversation listing is a prefix scan; channel-wide observability scans use the channel prefix. (2) Eager conversation-id reservation: active_conversation_id is guaranteed at session creation (restore-last or reserve-new), required because R16 mints the turn key at logical-turn start; deployments that never rotate conversations operate in a single implicit conversation. (3) Switch policy = AUTO-CANCEL: verified that neither /new_conversation (__main__.py:1174) nor /activate_conversation (:1349) checks awaiting_user or the pending store today. Decision: when a turn is suspended, both endpoints first cancel it - record the partial turn under its ORIGINAL conversation with status=cancelled (R39 semantics), clean up pending blob and suspend-offloaded payloads (R24) - then switch. Carry-across rejected (diverges agent memory from store under A1); 409-block rejected (UX). (4) Implementation note: cancel-then-switch must run under the per-session lock, which these endpoints do not currently acquire. Recorded in design doc Amendment A2 and review doc R9.
notes: Q and A: switch-while-suspended policy = auto-cancel (recommended option). Eager id reservation and channel-prefix listing settled without user input (conventional defaults). Cross-refs: R39 cancelled status, R24 orphan cleanup, R16 key minting.
close_reason: Finding finalized: structural namespace via A1, eager id reservation, auto-cancel on switch; recorded in bead, review doc R9, design doc Amendment A2

title: Add TTL/reaper for orphaned suspended-session blobs
description: In Topology B, when an agent suspends on ask_user and the user never returns, the durable pending-state blob persists indefinitely. SessionStateStore.save is called on suspend and on LRU eviction (eviction only SAVES, never deletes), and there is no TTL or background reaper. The only cleanup path is the /cancel_pending endpoint (ctx.cancel_pending() + store.clear()). Over time, abandoned blobs accumulate in DiskSessionStateStore (JSON files) and RedisSessionStateStore. Add a TTL/expiry mechanism: e.g. Redis key TTL on save, and a reaper/age-based sweep for the disk backend, with a configurable max-age. Ensure resuming refreshes the TTL.
acceptance_criteria: Abandoned pending blobs are eventually removed without manual cancel_pending; TTL is configurable; resuming an active suspension refreshes its expiry; tests cover expiry on both disk and redis backends.
notes:
Surfaced during trajectory-serialization review. Related: fastworkflow/session_state_store.py, fastworkflow/run_fastapi_mcp/utils.py (persist_pending_after_turn, _evict_oldest_if_needed).
Reaper contract addition from R24 final confirmation (2026-06-11): when deleting a stale pending blob, also delete the in-flight turn's payload prefix (fw:payload:{channel}:{conv}:{turn_key}:*) - trivial under A8 turn-scoped keys.

title: R30: Define user-facing vs developer-facing record projections
description:
FINDING: review records will contain framework-internal executions (command_metadata_extraction clarification commands are captured per 3.1), raw parameters and error detail; end-users and developers need different views. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) stamp internal: bool (or rely on workflow_name) per CommandOutput - schema decision needed now; (2) define the user projection (app commands + exchanges + answer) vs the full developer record. design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar). (1) ONE RECORD, TWO READ-TIME VIEWS - a projection function beside the record reader (A21); no dual storage. (2) INTERNAL DETECTION: DERIVED FROM workflow_name (user decision; no schema flag). The predicate is centralized in EXACTLY ONE PLACE - the projection function - so future internal execution sources have a single update point; the accepted caveat (read side carries the name contract) documented there. (3) VIEW COMPOSITIONS: user view = non-internal executions (app commands + ask_user exchanges per A7) + answer, status, success, user_message, gallery payloads; failed executions show error MESSAGE never traceback. Developer view = full record incl. internal CME executions, raw parameters, tracebacks (A5), trajectory_ref (A37), refined_user_message (A36). Helper-tool consultations (what_can_i_do, intent_misunderstood) are trajectory-only, noted in projection docs. Recorded in design doc Amendment A39 and review doc R30.

title: R28: Reserve a trajectory slot in review records (roadmap: durable agent trajectory)
description:
FINDING: the DSPy ReAct trajectory (react_blob) is already serialized for suspends (workflow_execution_context.py:270) and discarded at completion. Persisting it into the review record (PayloadStore-offloaded) is nearly free and directly serves the externalize-trajectory roadmap item; the R1 event sequence gives trajectory entries a natural home. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) reserve trajectory_ref now vs persist trajectory immediately; (2) size/PII considerations (interacts with R7). See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R28.

design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar) - PERSIST NOW. At every terminal write of an agent turn, the TurnSerializer (A21) offloads the ReAct trajectory to the turn-scoped PayloadStore (A8) and stamps TurnResult.trajectory_ref. Details: failed/cancelled/abandoned partials include the TRAJECTORY-SO-FAR (the crash evidence a why-did-it-fail investigation wants); developer projection only (R30); same retention, co-GC, and record-mediated access as every payload (A8/A12); deterministic and action turns carry None. The durable-trajectory roadmap item is thereby STORAGE-COMPLETE - its remaining work is consuming trajectories (memory/learning), not plumbing. Recorded in design doc Amendment A37 and review doc R28.

title: R23: Completion-sequence atomicity and review-write failure semantics
description:
FINDING: offload -> put record -> clear pending -> return has undefined failure semantics. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) review-write failure does NOT fail the turn (best-effort, loud log, monitored) - confirm and add the monitoring expectation; (2) crash-window analysis with R16 stable keys; (3) bound retries inside the per-session lock or move the write post-lock (ordinal still assigned in-lock per R18). See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R23.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar). (1) WRITE FAILURE = BEST-EFFORT + LOUD TELEMETRY: bounded in-line retries, then the turn succeeds for the user regardless - by record-write time the command's side effects are committed; erroring would invite double-execution retries. Failure logged prominently; a MONITORING COUNTER is documented as a deployment alarm expectation (post-A1 stakes acknowledged: a lost write means the turn is missing from conversation history and post-restart memory - the gap is operator-visible by contract). (2) CRASH WINDOWS = BOUNDED LEAKS (stated): payloads under a recordless turn prefix are adopted idempotently by a retry (A22 stable key + A27 hashed leaves) or removed eventually by conversation-prefix retention (A12). (3) STALE-PENDING SAFETY NET (stated): order = record write -> pending clear; at restore, a pending blob whose turn key already has a record is stale and discarded - record-write wins always (blob carries its turn key per A22). (4) LOCK HOLD (stated): write stays in-lock with a tight retry budget (sub-ms/ms writes vs multi-second turns); post-lock writing noted as a permitted future optimization (A24 assigns the ordinal in-lock either way). Recorded in design doc Amendment A28 and review doc R23.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 with the post-A1 stakes shift and the double-execution trap): best-effort + loud telemetry, recommended option. The side-effects-already-committed argument was decisive.
close_reason: Finding finalized: best-effort record writes with alarm-contract telemetry, bounded crash leaks, stale-pending discard rule, in-lock writes; recorded in bead, review doc R23, design doc Amendment A28

title: R13: Link assistant-mode clarification turns; correct the 5.5 invariant
description:

design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar). (1) continuation_of: Optional[turn_key] ADDED to TurnResult/the record, stamped when a turn begins in a correction state (NLU_Pipeline_Stage already tracks it; the predecessor's key is noted in the workflow context when the error turn ends). The exactly-one-CommandOutput claim holds only for the simple no-clarification case. Recorded in design doc Amendment A33 (with the 5.5 in-place correction note) and review doc R13.

title: R12: Resolve nested_turn open issues (producer, arity, suspension, semantics)
description:
FINDING: nested_turn has zero producers today (the same speculative generality the design kills command_responses for); singular may be wrong (a command can run multiple sub-turns); nested suspension (child ask_user inside parent partial) is undefined for serialize_state; nested user_message semantics undefined; embedded-only records grow unboundedly. OPEN QUESTIONS: (1) ship with a synthetic test producer; (2) nested_turns list vs Optional single; (3) define nested suspension serialization; (4) define nested user_message; (5) set a revisit trigger for embedded-only (p95 record size). See docs/turn_result_design_review.md R12.

MISSION (review, not implement): resolve the OPEN QUESTIONS collaboratively with the user, then finalize this finding into an unambiguous, detailed and complete issue description plus a design recommendation of matching quality. Record outcomes by updating this bead and the finding in docs/turn_result_design_review.md, and reconcile the affected sections of docs/turn_result_design.md. No code changes.
design: RESOLVED 2026-06-11 (with Dhar). (1) ARITY = LIST: CommandOutput.nested_turns: list[TurnResult] = [] (supersedes the singular nested_turn of design section 5.2) - empty in the common case, represents loops/fan-outs over sub-agents, never needs a record-schema break later. (2) NESTED SUSPENSION = ALWAYS ESCALATE: an inner agent's question bubbles up to become the TOP-LEVEL turn's suspension, generalizing the existing wildcard.py:61-71 escalation pattern. Consequence: a nested TurnResult attaches to its command ONLY WHEN COMPLETE; partial-inside-partial pending blobs never exist; all suspend/resume machinery (A19, A30) works unchanged at any nesting depth. Deliberately constrains future nested-agent designs to escalation-style clarification (also the simplest UX - one question stream). (3) STATED CONVENTIONS: nested user_message = the delegation instruction the parent command passed to the sub-agent; a SYNTHETIC TEST PRODUCER ships with the seam (R35 matrix) so the recursive serializer is never dead untested code; embedded-only revisit trigger named (p95 record size over ~256KB with real nested agents -> promote nested turns to references). Recorded in design doc Amendment A32 and review doc R12.
notes: Q and A (after ELI5 turn-within-a-turn): list arity + always-escalate, both recommended options. The escalation model's payoff: zero new suspension machinery at any nesting depth.
close_reason: Finding finalized: nested_turns list, escalation-only suspension, test producer + conventions; recorded in bead, review doc R12, design doc Amendment A32

title: Add schema migration for serialized session state (schema_version)
description: serialize_state writes schema_version (currently 1) and apply_serialized_state only logs a warning on mismatch; there is no migration logic. If the serialized blob format evolves, existing pending blobs (disk JSON / Redis) will be misread or silently dropped. Add versioned migration: on load, detect schema_version and upgrade older blobs to the current shape (or explicitly discard with a clear policy).
acceptance_criteria: Loading an older-schema blob either migrates it to the current schema or is handled by an explicit documented policy; mismatch no longer silently warns; tests cover an older-version blob.
notes: Surfaced during trajectory-serialization review. Related: fastworkflow/session_state_store.py (SCHEMA_VERSION), fastworkflow/workflow_execution_context.py apply_serialized_state.

title: Restore full command-context navigation depth on rehydration
description: serialize_state captures only current_command_context_name (a string). On cold rehydration, startup is re-run which resets to the root context; if the user had navigated several levels deep into a nested context, that exact position is not reconstructed. apply_serialized_state only logs a debug warning when the rehydrated name differs from the saved one. Design a way to serialize and restore the navigation path/position (e.g. an ordered list of context identifiers + a workflow-specific restore hook) so deep navigation survives relocation.
acceptance_criteria: A session navigated N levels deep resumes at the same context after cold rehydration on a fresh process; covered by an integration test using a nested-context workflow.
notes: Surfaced during trajectory-serialization review. Related: fastworkflow/workflow_execution_context.py serialize_state/apply_serialized_state, tests/test_command_context_serialization_spike.py.
